# Instructions

| In its most basic form, a gospel is a story centered around a prophetic figure, a person sent by God to simultaneously *criticize* the status quo and to *proclaim* hope for its eventual transformation. The word “gospel” is often translated into “good news” because it is intended to inspire hope in whoever would receive it. However, a prophet also brings a message that challenges the powers that be to look at themselves in the mirror and be painfully aware of their failures and their misgivings. It is thus unsurprising that whenever a prophet is sent, he is often rejected by the communities he is sent to. After all, it is not an easy thing to come face to face with one’s responsibilities. Prophets in the Scripture are persecuted, imprisoned, tortured and executed for the message that they proclaim, no least of which would be Jesus himself, who after a very short ministry, was sentenced to be crucified at the insistence of his enemies. It is therefore the intention of this assessment to give students an opportunity to also be the prophets and the protagonists of their own gospels. They can make use of any creative medium (a written essay, an artwork, a comic strip, a short film, etc.) to tell their stories, for as long as these projects can be saved as a .pdf, .jpeg, .mp3, or .mp4 file and uploaded on Canvas.  In the interest of storage space, physical projects like sculptures, papier-mâché, physical storybooks, etc. are not allowed. Students are encouraged to be as creative as they can possibly be, but they are also requested to ask for permission first if they intend to submit projects that cannot be uploaded to Canvas.   Part 1: Context Students must first select a social issue that they wish to respond to in their projects. They are encouraged to make use of their immersion experience in NSTP 11, but they can also discuss a different social issue of their choosing. In any case, they must be specific with regards to what issue they want to work on. For example, while racial discrimination is a good and urgent topic to discuss, the student must specify what instance of racial discrimination they wish to talk about. Does the student want to talk about discrimination towards Asians, African Americans, or Jews? Or is there any other instance of discrimination that would be more appealing to the student? These questions need to be answered first before anything else. Next, the students must now be able to tell a story that captures faithfully the facts of their chosen social issue. They can make use of figurative storytelling to do so, but it must still be based on facts. It is important that their artwork be able to demonstrate that they have done adequate research on their chosen social issue. They can, for example, make use of a fictional character living in the slums of New York, and tell a story of how this person has experienced discrimination on multiple levels. They cannot, however, invent details that are misrepresentative of reality.   Part 2: Action The student must then be able to insert himself/herself imaginatively into the story. There must be a figure there that represents them as an active participant in the narrative. It is also expected that this figure would respond in a way that is theologically appropriate, that is, that this figure would respond in ways that resonate with Gospel values and the principles of Catholic social thought. They can be as creative as they want to be in portraying themselves. To follow the example in the previous paragraph, they can either portray themselves as a friend to the character who has experienced discrimination, or they can even go as far as to portray themselves as some kind of a fictional character coming to his/her rescue. It is important to note that this basic structure is the minimum requirement for the project. It can be presented in any way that the student chooses, but the dynamic between context and action must always be present regardless of the chosen medium. |
| :---- |

checklist.png   **S U B M I S S I O N   R E Q U I R E M E N T S**

| paper.png PAPER SIZE | A4 paper, 1-inch margin on all sides, maximum of 4 pages (for those submitting a written essay) |
| :---: | :---- |
| alphabet.png  **FONT SIZE** | **n/a** |
| submit.png  **FILE TYPE** | Submit as a **.pdf file, .jpg file, .mp3, .mp4 file (depends on the medium chosen by the student)** |
| deadline.png  **DEADLINES** | April 25, 2026 (12 noon, via Canvas) |

**![][image1]**

# Ideas

1. Social Media Feed  
   1. Making a frontend only website  
   2. People bully and bully, and then when omehthing happens to the person they’re bullying, they point fingers and switch sides  
   3. Direct Social Issue: Financially benefitting from exploiting a famous guy’s weakness  
   4. Solution: theologically appropriate, that is, that this figure would respond in ways that resonate with Gospel values and the principles of Catholic social though  
   5. Must portray someone myself

Trends from the past (celebrity culture, which is still prevalent today) are carrying over to the digital stage (toxic idolization, hate-farming, etc.)

# Implementation Plan

# Structure

**Overall Situation**  
Add nuance. Paint the situation as morally gray. Perhaps the influencer was encouraging fans to “idolize” him in the name of attention and money. By pretending that he was single, he could attract a more devoted audience\*

A famous influencer gets outed for having a secret family by the side. The drama comes as a “whistleblower” doxxed the influencer and drama creators immediately pounced on it. The framing was that the influencer was pretending to be a single man and people that were parasocial felt that they were betrayed, that he had kept that detail all to himself (some even grieved the loss of their “celebrity crush”). A big drama channel with a “good reputation” covered the topic and it instantly became viral.

The influencer went silent for a while. Then, it became known that he and his family were violently attacked by his extreme fans in their own home. His children were hurt and traumatised and his wife was in critical condition. He made a long statement about it and lashed out at the public, vowing that he would quit being an influencer from then on.

The drama creators immediately backpedaled and criticized the extremists, upholding their own “moral superiority” even though they were the ones who stoked the fire in the first place. The big drama channel can be the biggest example for this. Sending prayers. Whilst also warning about the dangers of parasocialness.

I, the prophet (humorously mirroring Penguinz0 perhaps), calls out this hypocrisy, acknowledging the situation objectively, pointing out the nuances, and then upholding the “True” moral ground based on catholic stuff. Still, he gets massive hate for it online. He replies in kind, allowing for the expounding of the topic.

**CST Principles:**

1. Dignity of the human person: As the bedrock principle of CST, the principle of human dignity teaches that every human being is created in the image and likeness of God (Imago Dei) and is therefore is valuable, endowed with a dignity that is both inviolable and inalienable.  
   \- Just because the influencer intentionally was manipulative, that doesn’t give anyone the right to hurt him and his family.

2. Integral Human Development: we must allow people to actualize themselves, grow with their own efforts, not just giving them what they need. The principle of integral human development means that authentic development is integral: not limited to mere economic growth, promoting the good of every person and the whole person.  
   \-They must have allowed the influencer to actually reply to their accusations and apologize

3. Structures of Sin, the “Cult of Having” and the "Failure of Being” \- drama channels strive to be popular, wealthy, and successful, so they sensationalize events at the cost of spiritual values (respect for others, hypocrisy, etc.)  
     
4. Dignity of Human Labor \- In affirming the dignity of human labor, the Church teaches that work is an essential means by which a person shares in God's creative activity in the world. Therefore, work is for the person and not the person for the work.  
   Since it is the human person that labors, he must do so under circumstances that keep his integrity intact. He therefore has a right to work if he wants to and is able. Meaning that the powers that be must provide him with the opportunities to work. He must also be adequately compensated for the work that he has done. Finally, he must be given the opportunity to protect his rights in the workplace.   
   \-The influencer lost his job because

5. Common Good- Common Good Counterbalances Human Dignity: The common good is understood as the sum total of the social conditions that allow people to reach their full human potential and to realize their human dignity. Therefore our well-being is experienced amidst a setting in which other persons also flourish. Not collectivism or individualism, but a middleground  
   \-good of all human beings and to the whole human being  
     
6. Solidarity \-  human person, together with the human family, “is obliged to contribute to the common good of society at all its levels”  
     
7. Preferential Option for the Poor \- a call addressed to everyone to help build a community committed to the care of the weak and vulnerable. Its starting point is compassion for our fellow persons in need. It is not about antagonizing the rich but empowering them to understand and engage in actions WITH the poor towards greater outcomes  
     
8. Subsidiarity \- individual assistance and governmental assisstance; the ones in the top must not interfere what can be done in the lowest level; Placing responsibility as close as possible to the grassroots, the principle of subsidiarity teaches that the people/groups most directly affected by a decision or policy should have a key decision-making role.  
     
9. Participation \- moral duty of citizens to contribute—individually or in association with others— to the cultural, economic, political, and social life of the community to which they belong. Every human person has a duty and a right to participation \- that is, everyone must be able to enter into community, benefit from the community, and make a contribution worthy of their dignity as human beings.  
-  People must not just be bystanders and they must call out the situation and hold people accountable (drama creators etc)  
    
10. Integrity of Creation \- In loving creation into existence, God intends creation, the sign of God’s agape, to be whole.  Anything that leads to severe imbalances and destroys this fundamental wholeness is against the will of God. Environment stuff.  
      
11. Universal Destination of Goods \- The Church recognizes the right to private property as it is in keeping with the dignity of human persons to have the freedom to own and enjoy the fruits of their labors. However, the Church also teaches that the right to private property, never absolute and inviolable in its own, is subordinate to the right of everyone to the goods of the earth.  
- Pointing out the effects of attention economy on both sides

**UI:**  
The app’s name will be SocHub. The logo must look like similar to twitter’s x logo, but instead use s (in the same styling)

The layout and design of the entire app must exactly be similar to dark mode twitter UI. Implement verified badges on every poster except Zhongli’s Chair. That and the commenters must be unverified as well. It must be mobile responsive (again mirror twitter UI) as well as interaction responsive (hover states and anything typical of the twitter UI)  
The only differentiator would be reddit elements such as longform text, video embed, and upvotes/downvotes to emphasize reactions. Match the styles still.

This frontend app must only have two static routes (hardcode it), /feed/search?query=\#YaniDrama and /post/104856222992415

The twitter UI elements may be there (share, home, notifications, etc), but lets just make sure that they dont do anything (no redirects), except for the upvote/downvotes. Make up random numbers for them for now, I’ll adjust them later. Just make sure that user can interact with them. Use useState for the upvote/downvote buttons so that if I click the upvote, the number increments by 1 and the arrow turns orange/red, and if I click downvote it turns blue. It doesn't need to persist on refresh.

The only interactions available to the user must be scrolling through the feed, interacting with the posts (votes), clicking on the prophetic post to go into the hardcoded route of the post (as well as going back into the feed using a back button up top), and interacting with the comments section (they can post their own messages as well, just dont save them so we dont need a backend).   
As for the “video embeds”, just leave placeholder images (next Image) with dimensions that match the UI (specify the dimensions, so that I just have to replace the src attribute) and make sure they’re not clickable. I plan to have them just be static. This applies to other images as well (profile pictures, apology picture, etc.). 

Build the posts according to what I have written and specified in the content section.

**Structure:**

Main feed:

* Search Bar at top: \#YaniDrama  
* List of Posts  
  * Initial expose  
  * Repost of big channel creator, has his own branded expose post with text (mirror keemstar)  
  * Another drama creator emphasizing on cheap thumbnails with “its over”, “manipulator”  
  * News story: family attacked  
  * Parasocial fan saying thoughts and prayers but still attacking the creator; “they deserve it”  
  * Prophethic post

Prophetic post

* default link for submission  
* massive back button is at the top-left corner  
* Video link with thumbnail is included in the post, but the author goes in text about the whole situation anyways  
* Describe the situation in full.  
* Comments section should be filled with hate comments

  Crazed Fans Attack Internet Celebrity “Yani” in Own Home With Family

In the end, all suspects were apprehended and taken into custody. After initial questioning, the attack is found to be apparently motivated by Yani’s “betrayal” of their most devoted supporters. We have yet to receive a statement from the victims.  
  9M ^ | 2K downvotes  
    
  * TeamStarr  
    This is disgusting.  
    Sending my deepest prayers to Adrian's family right now. Violence is never the answer. I always warn you guys about the dangers of parasocial relationships... Go f\*cking touch grass y’all and pray for his family.  
    5M ^ | 132K downvotes		

	

* Streamer’s response  
  Yani  
  Goodbye. (title)  
  White Bg Image with text (reminiscent of typical apology posts):  
  I apologize to everyone I may hurt through my actions.  
    
  I’ve done you all wrong, and I’m not asking for anyone’s forgiveness.  
    
  The attack on us was absolutely revolting and completely unjustifiable.  
  My children have been scarred from the experience.  
  I don’t even know if my wife can make it through tonight.  
  I have had enough. Please spare my family from this. They have nothing to do with all that I’ve done.   
    
  I am deleting all of my channels. Thanks to everyone who has supported me this far. Despite everything, I want you all to know that I really appreciate you guys. Goodbye.  
  2M ^ | 1M downvotes

  * Parasocial fan saying thoughts and prayers but still attacking the creator;   
    Zhongli’s Chair  
    What happened to @Yani was bad for sure. But with all of the the disgusting sh\*t he’s done, I’d say he deserved it all.  
    132 ^ | 6 downvotes  
      
  * Prophethic post  
    DryKritkal  
    About the whole Yani situation… (title)  
      
    Fake Embed (static image)  
    

It’s all an absolute mess.

Let’s just get the obvious out of the way first: Yani is a sorry excuse of a human being. Pretending to be a single “e-boy” streamer to milk donations from lonely, parasocial viewers is straight up disgusting. For God’s sake, he’s even reached out to people pretending to be struggling and asking for money. It's greedy, it's manipulative, and he absolutely deserves to get hate on that.

That said, the way the internet has reacted to all the recent news is just so wrong in so many ways.

Now, I’ve been reading up on some Catholic Social Teachings lately. Normally, I don’t get philosophical here, but I just happened to be in this rabbit hole right now and thought that I could spread some words of wisdom here for y’all with them.

First off, drama channels like TeamStarr doxxing him and turning his life into a damn circus worship the **"Cult of Having”.** They’re willing to throw human lives into a meatgrinder if it means getting a few extra clicks and ad revenue. They suffer from a complete "Failure of Being." Then suddenly now that Yani's wife is in the ICU fighting for her life, these same troglodytes have the audacity to tweet 'thoughts and prayers' like they didn't just hand the mob the pitchforks and flaming torches in the first place.

Then there's the **Life and Dignity of the Human Person**. Just because Yani is a liar and a manipulator doesn't mean you get to strip him of his humanity and launch a goddamn siege on his house. We all have our own dignity. It's *Imago Dei*. You don't lose your fundamental right to physical safety just because you scammed and lied to people.

Also, since when did everyone suddenly become executioners? We didn’t even let him have the chance to address all this. We didn't allow for **Integral Human Development**. We didn't want him to grow or make amends; the whole internet mob just wanted the drama. They wanted blood.

Worse yet, we've turned the internet into a digital cockfighting ring.. Its platforms should serve the **Common Good**, not be playgrounds for drama channels to hoard wealth by exploiting human suffering. If we actually care about **Solidarity**, we have a moral obligation to stop validating the algorithms that do this.

Stop clicking the clickbait. Unsubscribe. Report these bastards.

The good news is that we don't have to live in this bubble. We can pop it. We have the power to build a digital community based on empathy, where we hold people accountable without destroying them. We can start praying for each other instead of preying on each other.

240K ^ | 478K downvotes

You can't toss a grenade into a room, monetize the explosion, and then act like an innocent journalist. That is exactly what a **Structure of Sin** is.

👍 2.4k | 👎 2

↳ **@DryKritkal** \[PROPHET\] • 15 mins ago  
The **Preferential Option for the Poor** isn't just about who has less or more money; it’s a call to care for the vulnerable. And, right now, traumatized kids and a woman fighting for her life in an ICU are the most vulnerable people in this whole thing. Compassion isn't conditional on someone's net worth. Having a nice house and a big wallet doesn't make you immune to physical violence.  
👍 5 | 👎 300

↳ **@DryKritkal** \[PROPHET\] • 5 mins ago  
Relying purely on top-down bans violates the principle of **Subsidiarity**. Responsibility needs to be placed as close to the grassroots as possible—that means us. We have to uphold the **Integrity of Creation**, which tells us not to pollute the internet, which is an environment we all share, with toxic garbage. Remember, we’re the ones feeding the algorithm.
